courses.title: Soil Fundamentals
courses.description: Understand soil types, structure, and how to improve soil health for better yields.

lessons.title: Soil Layers Explained
lessons.content: Soil has distinct layers: topsoil (rich in organic matter), subsoil (minerals), and bedrock. Healthy topsoil is dark and crumbly.
lessons.intuition: Think of soil like a layered cake—the top layer is what feeds your crops.

questions.question: Which layer is richest in organic matter?
questions.explanation: Topsoil contains decomposed plant and animal matter, making it the most fertile layer.

questions.question: What does subsoil mainly provide?
questions.explanation: Subsoil holds water and minerals that roots can draw from during dry periods.

lessons.title: Soil Texture
lessons.content: Soil texture depends on sand, silt, and clay proportions. Loam—a balance of all three—is ideal for most crops.
lessons.intuition: Sand drains fast, clay holds water; loam gives you the best of both.

questions.question: Which soil type drains water fastest?
questions.explanation: Sand has large particles and large pores, so water passes through quickly.

lessons.title: Organic Matter
lessons.content: Adding compost and crop residues increases organic matter. This improves water retention and nutrient availability.
lessons.intuition: More organic matter means a happier, more productive soil.

questions.question: What benefit does organic matter provide?
questions.explanation: Organic matter improves both nutrient availability and the soil's ability to hold water.

courses.title: Crop Planning
courses.description: Plan seasons, choose crops, and rotate for sustainable production.

lessons.title: Kharif vs Rabi
lessons.content: Kharif crops are sown with monsoon (e.g. rice, maize). Rabi crops are winter crops (e.g. wheat, mustard).
lessons.intuition: Match your crop to the season—water and temperature matter.

questions.question: When are Rabi crops typically sown?
questions.explanation: Rabi crops are winter-season crops, sown after monsoon and harvested in spring.

courses.title: Irrigation & Water Management
courses.description: Efficient water use, drip and sprinkler basics, and when to irrigate.

lessons.title: How Water Moves in Soil
lessons.content: Water moves downward by gravity and upward by capillary action. Roots absorb water from the root zone.
lessons.intuition: Water follows the path of least resistance—design irrigation to match root depth.

questions.question: What pulls water downward in soil?
questions.explanation: Gravity is the main force moving water downward through soil pores.

courses.title: Pest & Disease Awareness
courses.description: Identify common pests and diseases and learn preventive practices.

lessons.title: Signs of Pest Damage
lessons.content: Chewed leaves, holes, yellowing, and stunted growth can indicate pests. Early detection helps control spread.
lessons.intuition: Catch problems early—small damage is easier to manage than an outbreak.

questions.question: Where should you often check for pests?
questions.explanation: Many pests hide or lay eggs on the undersides of leaves.

courses.title: Farming Economics
courses.description: Costs, pricing, and simple record-keeping for better decisions.

lessons.title: Fixed vs Variable Costs
lessons.content: Fixed costs (land, equipment) don't change with output. Variable costs (seeds, labour) do. Both matter for profit.
lessons.intuition: Know your costs before you plant—then you know what price you need.

questions.question: Which cost changes with how much you grow?
questions.explanation: Variable costs like seeds, fertilizer, and labour increase when you grow more.
